channel.title: My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Unit3
item.description:
Reflecting on Failure in Group Work

In this unit, I learned about reflecting on failure through a group activity. This was a different experience for me because it was not only about studying, but also about working with others and understanding our mistakes.

During the group work, not everything went as expected. Sometimes we had different ideas, and it was difficult to manage time and divide the work equally. At first, I felt a bit uncomfortable because I wanted everything to go perfectly. But slowly, I understood that small failures are a normal part of teamwork.

item.title: Unit2
item.description:
My experience about binary and problem solving

In this unit, I felt easy at the beginning. Because binary numbers were already learned from 12th. So I feel easier.Since I had learned some basics before, I did not find the initial topics very difficult. However, as the unit went to deeper, I realized that my understanding was only at a surface level. When I tried to solving problems more carefully, I noticed that I still make small mistakes. This made me understand that knowing a topic and fully understanding it. I also realized that even if I already know a topic, I should not ignore practice. When I revised and solved problems again, my understanding became clearer and stronger. This unit taught me to not be overconfident about what I already know. It is important to go deeper and check our understanding. Overall, this experience helped me improve my thinking and made my basics stronger.

item.title: Unit 1 Reflection
item.description:
Understanding What Information Really Means

Hey I’m Abdul Basith K T studying Information Science in Azim Premji University. In this unit, I learned about what information really means in Information Science. At first, I thought information was just data or facts. But this unit helped me understand that information is more than that. It depends on how we understand and use data.

One thing that changed my thinking was the DIKW model. It showed me that data, information, knowledge, and wisdom are all different. Before this, I used to think they were almost the same. Now I understand that data is just raw facts, and information is when we give meaning to that data.
